author: @eelcohoogendoorn8044
text: Probably doesnt short because the tough (PET?) plastic extrudes around the nail along the hole. Then again thats not an accident and exactly what these plastic foils do consistently when you try to puncture them; so definitely a feature compared to more volatile batteries!

author: @RWBHere
text: The battery which you can staple to a workbench! Hmm.... but it's still awesome. What fire hazards are there? Paper, Carbon, electrolyte, shorting? It will be interesting to explore this avenue. Maybe the inherent relative safety can be used as a selling point. Thanks Robert.

author: @ionelfanel
text:
Hello, and welcome back no new videos I missed your videos, I learned so many things from you, I am fascinated by the materials that can accumulate energy, I learned why it happens because simple materials are so wonderful.

I learned a lot from your videos, even British English:) thank you for sharing your knowledge with us.

I wish you much health, and so many beautiful things, you are the best.


Ciao,e bentornato non nuovi video mi sono mancati gli tuoi video,ho imparato tante cose da da te,sono affascinato dagli materiali che possono accumulare energia,ho imparate perché succede perché semplici materiali sono cosi meravigliosi.

Ho imparato da vero tante cose dagli tuoi video ,anche l inglese britannico:)grazie per aver condiviso le tue conoscenze con noi .

Ti auguro tanta salute,e tante belle coso ,sei il migliore.

author: @jims9895
text:
commercial idea...
scene1: troops in a Humvee doing recon somewhere undisclosed with communications gear in tow...


scene2: the camera slowly zooms in on the FWG battery pack through the dust being kicked up by the drive...


scene3: enemy combatants appear over a ridge with a Gatling gun and unleash a hailstorm of bullets while the coms specialist is trying to report their position.


scene4: the enemy is destroyed in a huge blast because "explosions are awesome"


scene5: soldiers are going over the damage writing up the report in the aftermath


scene6: the coms specialist calls the sergeant and says with a confused look on his face..."it still works, even after they turned the battery to swiss cheese i was still able to call in their location sir!... it still works! these batteries are amazing sir!!"


scene7: the FWG battery becomes the official battery for the UK armed forces, the US armed forces, the Olympics and NASA...


and because i'm awesome you'll send me a 120 volt set up for the awesome idea... ;<]

author: @technosaurus3805
text:
Those Tesla solar cell roofing tiles could integrate these without worrying about botched installation deaths... or any solar panels for that matter... not just in a Twizzy.

I know you toyed with 18650 format, but it seemed like the cell voltage is significantly different than lithium (and now sodium and potassium) cells.  With that in mind, filling a different niche could be much easier from a marketing perspective.  The ability to make batteries of any size, shape and capacity let's you start with higher end  integrated design such as:

Fill a wall/ceiling cavity for off grid designs that don't want an ugly battery bank.
Frame integrated ebike batteries.
Body integrated car/truck batteries.
Lower profile autonomous mowers.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i don't know why folks want us to compete against lithium - we aren't - lithium accounts for only 35% of the battery market - lead acid is 55% - that's what we are after.

author: @DanA-nl5uo
text: That is a great test.  Can you repeat it monitoring the temperature?  If you can verify there is no change in temperature it would go a long ways twords the issue of fire risk in an accident.  Thinking of the EV industry one big push behind solid state batteries is the fire risk from lithium ion batteries.  Which is already lower then the fire risk from ICE cars.

author: @rouzbehtorkisharifabadi1639
text:
greattttttttt :)
do you use Aluminium correct collector for both electrodes? don't you have problem to get 2 volts with the same current collectors?

replies.author: @cayrex
replies.text:
For the supercapacitor type is not a problem to get 2 volts, is all in electrolyte. For a battery type is one material different,.... most of the time is the anode. For example,... if robert want to change from capacitor type to battery,... he change a anode material or changing the cathode works also.
Current collectors are only plates to collect the current and most of the time the current collectors dont effect the system.

author: @ZeroInDaHouse
text: You mentioned these cells to be around 40-50 Wh/kg , if Li-ion is around 200Wh/kg and going higher every year what will be the main competing factor (besides price)  of these cells compared to Li-ion batteries? I know high numbers look more sexy but wanted your take on this. Thanks and keep up the great work.

replies.author: @William_Hada
replies.text: Did you actually watch the whole video? Try driving a nail through a lithium battery sometime and see what happens!  Considering the hazardous history of lithium batteries, I would think safety would be a huge advantage over them.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: price and safety mate - that's all it needs to be for our target market - in home and grid storage cost is king and safety is paramount - the rest just doesn't matter

replies.author: @ZeroInDaHouse
replies.text: ​@ThinkingandTinkering Thanks, indeed for home use where space is not much of a concern rather than price and safety this would be ideal. In fact I see the potential to be much higher because the initial cells are literally hand made and improving one component of the manufacturing process can significantly improve  energy density. Again keep up the great work, watching every update with great excitement.

replies.author: @Barskor1
replies.text: Using graphene rather than tire carbon IMO drastically improves the power to weight ratio. The battery on test here is the cheapest safest thing possible and still be utilitarianly viable.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: thank you mate and you are right there is a ton of improvement to be made -to be honest we prettymuch have it all stacked against us - and still it is performing - that's just awesome as far as i am concerned
